question: How do you know about Serial port connector (10-1 pin COM) of a motherboard?
content: The 10-1 pin COM connector is the motherboard header for a serial (COM) port. Connect the serial port module cable to this COM header, then install the module in a slot opening at the back of the system chassis; the COM module itself is purchased separately. The illustration shows the motherboard location and the COM header/pin layout for that cable connection.<PIC>
ret: "The 10-1 pin COM connector is the motherboard header for a serial (COM) port. Connect the serial port module cable to this COM header, then install the module in a slot opening at the back of the system chassis; the COM module itself is purchased separately. The illustration shows the motherboard location and the COM header/pin layout for that cable connection.<PIC>",["Manual25_39"]
notes: Answered from the Motherboard internal connectors section. The adjacent figure is used because it shows the COM header location and pin layout for the serial port module cable.
